mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equities and reduces near-term risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average, residential investment is contracting and equity investors mark down cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST downside crossings have mean 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional mean in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months signals labor-market deterioration and expected earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months
regime_hint: transition from expansion to labor weakening
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are not followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS with UNRATE below 5% signals household income and spending momentum that lifts expected revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0 while UNRATE < 5%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term has nonpositive walk-forward coefficient or if triggered instances fail to beat unconditional 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS indicates overheating rather than benign production growth, compressing margins and valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 0, CPIAUCSL 3-month growth > 0, and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Reject if triggered overheating observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 excess returns versus unconditional returns with t-test p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDPC1 growth over the latest reported quarter signals broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot GDPC1=11233.5 as of 2005-Q4 information lag
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4% and CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is positive, the market prices additional policy restraint, reducing valuation multiples over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4% while CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show a positive average 5-day S&P 500 return or if the interaction coefficient is nonnegative in walk-forward estimates.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving production and near-term operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.
